plays.title: A Christmas Carol
plays.description: The reclamation of a tight-fisted calculator under the audit of three spirits and a chain-bound ghost.

plays.title: The Chimes
plays.description: A goblin story of bells that rang an old year out, auditing a ticket-porter's lost faith in humanity.

plays.title: The Cricket on the Hearth
plays.description: A fairy tale of home, staging the testing of a carrier's trust and the protective illusions of a poor toy-maker.

plays.title: The Battle of Life
plays.description: A love story, staging the quiet battle of self-sacrifice fought between two sisters on an ancient battlefield.

plays.title: The Haunted Man
plays.description: The ghost's bargain, staging a chemist's flight from memory and the moral infection of oblivion in a college town.

plays.title: The Pickwick Papers
plays.description: The travels of the Pickwickians, staging naive benevolence audited by a breach-of-promise suit and the Fleet Prison.

plays.title: Oliver Twist
plays.description: Staging the workhouse economy, Fagin's pickpocket academy, and Monks's plot to destroy Oliver's inheritance.

plays.title: Nicholas Nickleby
plays.description: Fortunes of a young gentleman, staging Yorkshire school economy, theatrical diversion, and Ralph's usury.

plays.title: The Old Curiosity Shop
plays.description: Pilgrimage of Nell and Grandfather, staging curiosity trade, usurious foreclosure, and country flight.

plays.title: Barnaby Rudge
plays.description: Gordon Riots of Eighty, staging locksmith duty, apprentice mutiny, and mob frenzy.

plays.title: Martin Chuzzlewit
plays.description: A story of selfishness and hypocrisy, staging architectural deception, speculative bubbles, and swampy moral reform.

plays.title: Dombey and Son
plays.description: Staging mercantile pride, corporate treachery, railway fate, and family reconciliation.

plays.title: David Copperfield
plays.description: A personal history of growth and memory, staging step-parent tyranny, fawning blackmail, and bohemian optimism.

plays.title: Bleak House
plays.description: A drama of legal delay, staging Chancery paralysis, litigant obsession, and detective investigation.

plays.title: Hard Times
plays.description: A drama of industrial labor, staging the conflict between Fact and Fancy, school fact-grinding, and a circus escape.

plays.title: Little Dorrit
plays.description: A drama of imprisonment and delay, staging the debtors' prison, Circumlocution Office, and speculation.

plays.title: A Tale of Two Cities
plays.description: A drama of resurrection and violence, staging the dual cities, Madame Defarge's register, and Carton's sacrifice.

plays.title: Great Expectations
plays.description: A drama of social ambition and class snobbery, staging Satis House decay, a convict benefactor, and the forge sanctuary.

plays.title: Our Mutual Friend
plays.description: A drama of wealth and rebirth, staging dust-heaps, a river scavenger economy, and Jenny Wren's dolls' workshop.

plays.title: The Mystery of Edwin Drood
plays.description: A drama of mystery and double lives, staging cathedral town shadows, John Jasper's drug-dreams, and Datchery's inquiry.

plays.title: Sketches by Boz
plays.description: A panoramic observation of London street life, parish beadle charity, and middle-class boarding houses.

plays.title: The Uncommercial Traveller
plays.description: A series of nocturnal walks, institutional inspections, and provincial memories across Victorian England.

plays.title: Christmas Stories
plays.description: A festive collection of winter charity, lodging-house adoptions, street auctions, and railway junction redemptions.

plays.title: Master Humphrey's Clock
plays.description: A framing narrative of clock club meetings, stored manuscripts, and the return of Samuel Pickwick and the Wellers.

plays.title: Holiday Romance
plays.description: A children's romance subverting grown-up rules through mock trials, magic fishbones, and pirate adventures.

plays.title: George Silverman's Explanation
plays.description: A melancholy monologue of cellar childhood, preacher hypocrisy, and quiet misunderstandings of self-sacrifice.

plays.title: The Mudfog Papers
plays.description: A parody of scientific vanity, staging a mayor's disastrous pageant and the absurd scientific exhibitions.
